label: Self-consistency & best-of-N inference

node.label: Self-consistency & best-of-N inference
node.definition: Self-consistency samples multiple reasoning trajectories and aggregates their answers, using diversity to reduce dependence on one brittle chain.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Self-consistency & best-of-N inference is modeled as depending on Chain-of-thought & latent reasoning, Recursive partitioning & ensembling and feeding downstream nodes such as Reflection, critique, verifier & repair loops, Test-time compute scaling, Best-of-N & verifier-guided selection.
examples: atlas downstream link to Reflection, critique, verifier & repair loops
atlas downstream link to Test-time compute scaling
atlas downstream link to Best-of-N & verifier-guided selection
limitations: Search and self-evaluation can multiply cost while preserving confident errors when states, tools, or verifiers are weak. Its direct dependencies include Chain-of-thought & latent reasoning, Recursive partitioning & ensembling.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, Self-consistency & best-of-N inference has a `uses` dependency on Chain-of-thought & latent reasoning because Chain-of-thought & latent reasoning is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.

In this atlas graph, Self-consistency & best-of-N inference has a `uses` dependency on Recursive partitioning & ensembling because Recursive partitioning & ensembling is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.

In this atlas graph, Self-consistency & best-of-N inference has a `uses` dependency on Search, sampling & decoding procedures because Search, sampling & decoding procedures is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
